payload.title: Unified Engine: pixel-image-not-discernible — Pixels should be hidden from assistive technology
payload.description: **Engine rule ID:** `pixel-image-not-discernible` **RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/pixel-image-not-discernible`, `/engine/pixel-image-not-discernible_success`, `/engine/pixel-image-not-discernible_failure` **Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/pixel-image-not-discernible/atomic-tests/` **Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902) ## Flow Phases ### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3) Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library. ### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4) Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations. ### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5) Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples. --- **Rule description:** Visually hidden pixel images (often used for analytics or marketing purposes) should not be announced by screen readers. **Remediation advice:** If the pixel image is an element, assign an empty alt attribute. For other cases, add role="none" or role="presentation" to the pixel element. **Fixtures:** 7 pass, 1 fail
payload.preconditions:
1. **RAIDEN** Engine Rules Library running (`BASE_URL` from project `.env`).
2. **core-engine-auditor** atomic test runner available for rule validation.
3. **AccessFlow** environment with website scan capability.
4. **Rule** `pixel-image-not-discernible` examples published at `/engine/pixel-image-not-discernible_success` and `/engine/pixel-image-not-discernible_failure`.
5. **Fixture count:** 7 pass, 1 fail atomic HTML files.
payload.postconditions:
1. All pass fixtures produce **passed nodes** in atomic audit and no false-positive issues in AccessFlow.
2. All fail fixtures produce **failed nodes** in atomic audit and matching issues in AccessFlow.
3. RAIDEN example pages match atomic fixture HTML and expected outcomes.

payload.title: Unified Engine: popup-focus — Keyboard focus should move into and lock inside active dialogs
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `popup-focus`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/popup-focus`, `/engine/popup-focus_success`, `/engine/popup-focus_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/popup-focus/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Dialogs that appear on pages without receiving keyboard focus immediately on interaction often leave users navigating content behind the dialog and make it difficult or impossible for keyboard and screen reader users to access the dialog itself.
**Remediation advice:** When a dialog opens, use JavaScript to place keyboard focus on the first interactive element within the dialog. If static content, such as lists, tables, or paragraphs, appears before any interactive elements and it needs to be perceived in order to easily understand the content, tabindex="-1" can be added to a static element at the start of the content to initially focus that element.
**Fixtures:** 0 pass, 0 fail
payload.preconditions:
1. **RAIDEN** Engine Rules Library running (`BASE_URL` from project `.env`).
2. **core-engine-auditor** atomic test runner available for rule validation.
3. **AccessFlow** environment with website scan capability.
4. **Rule** `popup-focus` examples published at `/engine/popup-focus_success` and `/engine/popup-focus_failure`.
5. **Fixture count:** 0 pass, 0 fail atomic HTML files.
payload.postconditions:
1. All pass fixtures produce **passed nodes** in atomic audit and no false-positive issues in AccessFlow.
2. All fail fixtures produce **failed nodes** in atomic audit and matching issues in AccessFlow.
3. RAIDEN example pages match atomic fixture HTML and expected outcomes.

payload.title: Unified Engine: radio-aria-checked — Checked state of custom radio controls should be indicated to assistive technology
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `radio-aria-checked`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/radio-aria-checked`, `/engine/radio-aria-checked_success`, `/engine/radio-aria-checked_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/radio-aria-checked/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** If custom radio controls lack a defined checked state, screen reader users can't determine which option is selected, resulting in confusion and loss of functionality.
**Remediation advice:** Assign \\
**Fixtures:** 3 pass, 1 fail

payload.title: Unified Engine: radio-discernible — Radio controls should have a label
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `radio-discernible`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/radio-discernible`, `/engine/radio-discernible_success`, `/engine/radio-discernible_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/radio-discernible/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Screen readers rely on properly coded and associated labels to announce the purpose of a form field. A radio control without an identifiable label may prevent screen reader users from completing the form.
**Remediation advice:** Assign a label to each radio control using <label for> with a matching id (or wrapping the <label> around the control), or ARIA attributes such as aria-label or aria-labelledby.
**Fixtures:** 11 pass, 6 fail
payload.preconditions:
1. **RAIDEN** Engine Rules Library running (`BASE_URL` from project `.env`).
2. **core-engine-auditor** atomic test runner available for rule validation.
3. **AccessFlow** environment with website scan capability.
4. **Rule** `radio-discernible` examples published at `/engine/radio-discernible_success` and `/engine/radio-discernible_failure`.
5. **Fixture count:** 11 pass, 6 fail atomic HTML files.
payload.postconditions:
1. All pass fixtures produce **passed nodes** in atomic audit and no false-positive issues in AccessFlow.
2. All fail fixtures produce **failed nodes** in atomic audit and matching issues in AccessFlow.
3. RAIDEN example pages match atomic fixture HTML and expected outcomes.

payload.title: Unified Engine: radio-mismatch — Custom radio controls should be tagged for assistive technology
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `radio-mismatch`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/radio-mismatch`, `/engine/radio-mismatch_success`, `/engine/radio-mismatch_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/radio-mismatch/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Screen readers have built-in mechanisms to handle radio components. By default, assistive technology does not support custom radio controls and using them without exposing the appropriate role may prevent screen reader users from interacting as expected with the component.
**Remediation advice:** Assign role="radio" to the custom radio control.
**Fixtures:** 5 pass, 2 fail
payload.preconditions:
1. **RAIDEN** Engine Rules Library running (`BASE_URL` from project `.env`).
2. **core-engine-auditor** atomic test runner available for rule validation.
3. **AccessFlow** environment with website scan capability.
4. **Rule** `radio-mismatch` examples published at `/engine/radio-mismatch_success` and `/engine/radio-mismatch_failure`.
5. **Fixture count:** 5 pass, 2 fail atomic HTML files.
payload.postconditions:
1. All pass fixtures produce **passed nodes** in atomic audit and no false-positive issues in AccessFlow.
2. All fail fixtures produce **failed nodes** in atomic audit and matching issues in AccessFlow.
3. RAIDEN example pages match atomic fixture HTML and expected outcomes.
